profile.behavior: Social and often seen in flocks year-round. Feeds mainly on fruit, swallowing berries whole or taking them during brief hovering flights; supplements its diet with flying insects in summer.
field_marks: Sleek, silky-plumaged songbird with a pointed, often swept-back crest
Narrow black face mask crisply edged in white
Warm tan-brown head and breast grading into soft gray wings and back
Pale yellow belly; short, square-tipped dark tail with a bright yellow terminal band
Short, broad dark bill and dark eye; dark legs
Some adults show small red wax-like tips on the secondary wing feathers
profile.habitat: Open deciduous, coniferous, or mixed woodlands; forest edges, old fields, orchards, parks, gardens, and other fruiting trees or shrubs, often near water.

sources.title: Cedar Waxwing Identification — Cornell Lab of Ornithology
Cedar Waxwing Life History — Cornell Lab of Ornithology
Cedar Waxwing — Smithsonian’s National Zoo and Conservation Biology Institute

findings: Verified identity: Cedar Waxwing (Bombycilla cedrorum), family Bombycillidae. The listed length, weight, and wingspan exactly match Cornell’s published measurements and Smithsonian independently supports approximately 6–7 in and 1.1 oz.

All visible factual text is supported: social flocking, fruit/berry feeding including brief hovering, summer aerial insect feeding, woodland/orchard/garden habitat, crest, narrow black-and-white mask, brown-to-gray plumage, pale yellow belly, short square-tipped dark tail with yellow terminal band, dark bill/eye/legs, and variable red waxy secondary tips.

The illustration shows one head, one beak, two legs with feet, one tail, and the expected two-wing anatomy (one wing shown fully, the far wing occluded). It matches the supplied reference photos in crest, mask, soft gray wings, yellow tail tip, and red secondary accents.

Minor illustration issue: the bill is rendered slightly longer and more sharply tapered than the characteristically short, wide Cedar Waxwing bill. This is not a material identification or text error.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears.
